# Singapore city guide

## Overview

Singapore is a tiny island nation that punches way above its weight. Impossibly clean, absurdly efficient, and home to one of the greatest food scenes on earth. Hawker centres serve Michelin-starred meals for $3, futuristic gardens glow at night, and the cultural blend of Chinese, Malay, Indian, and Peranakan influences makes every neighborhood feel distinct.

**Best time to visit**: Year-round (it's always hot and humid). Feb-Apr is slightly drier. Dec-Jan has the most rain but festive decorations.
**Currency**: Singapore Dollar (SGD). 1.35 SGD ≈ 1 USD.
**Language tip**: English is one of four official languages and widely spoken. Singlish (local English) adds "lah," "lor," and "can" to everything.

## Neighborhoods

| Neighborhood | Vibe | Best for | Transit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Marina Bay | Iconic, modern | Marina Bay Sands, ArtScience Museum, skyline views | MRT, Bayfront Station |
| Chinatown | Historic, foodie | Hawker food, temples, heritage shophouses | MRT, Chinatown Station |
| Little India | Colorful, aromatic | Indian food, Tekka Centre, flower garlands | MRT, Little India Station |
| Kampong Glam | Artsy, Malay-Arab | Sultan Mosque, Haji Lane street art, cafes | MRT, Bugis Station |
| Orchard Road | Shopping, glossy | Malls, department stores, luxury brands | MRT, Orchard Station |
| Tiong Bahru | Hip, residential | Brunch cafes, bakeries, art deco architecture | MRT, Tiong Bahru Station |
| Holland Village | Expat, relaxed | Casual dining, bars, weekend vibes | MRT, Holland Village Station |
| Sentosa Island | Resort, beach | Universal Studios, beaches, cable car | Sentosa Express from VivoCity |

## Food

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description | Must-order |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Liao Fan Hong Kong Soya Sauce Chicken Rice | Hawker (Michelin) | Chinatown | $ | The world's cheapest Michelin-starred meal. Tender soy sauce chicken over fragrant rice for about $3. | Soya sauce chicken rice |
| Maxwell Food Centre | Hawker centre | Chinatown | $ | Home to dozens of legendary stalls in one hawker centre. Come hungry, try everything. | Tian Tian chicken rice, Zhen Zhen porridge |
| Jumbo Seafood | Seafood | Clarke Quay/Multiple | $$-$$$ | The chili crab institution. Messy, spicy, sweet, and absolutely essential. Get mantou buns to soak up the sauce. | Chili crab with mantou buns |
| Tekka Centre | Hawker centre | Little India | $ | The best Indian food hawker centre. Biryani, roti prata, fish head curry, all under one roof. | Roti prata, fish head curry, biryani |
| 328 Katong Laksa | Noodles | Katong | $ | Rich, coconut-milk based laksa with prawns and fishcake. Served with a spoon only, noodles are pre-cut. | Katong laksa |
| Burnt Ends | Fine dining | Chinatown | $$$ | Australian-style wood-fired modern cuisine. One Michelin star, creative small plates from the fire. | Pulled pork brioche, marrow, king crab |
| Tiong Bahru Bakery | Bakery | Tiong Bahru | $$ | French-inspired bakery in a charming neighborhood. The kouign-amann is buttery perfection. | Kouign-amann, croissant, flat white |
| Zam Zam | Indian-Muslim | Kampong Glam | $ | Murtabak (stuffed roti) across from Sultan Mosque since 1908. Crispy, stuffed with mutton or chicken. | Mutton murtabak |
| Old Airport Road Food Centre | Hawker centre | Dakota | $ | Locals' favorite hawker centre away from tourist areas. Over 150 stalls, everything is good. | Fried hokkien mee, satay, rojak |
| Atlas | Bar/restaurant | Bugis | $$$ | Art Deco gin palace with one of the world's largest gin collections. The lobby alone is worth the visit. | Gin and tonic flight, champagne |

## Landmarks and activities

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Time needed | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Gardens by the Bay | Nature | Marina Bay | Free-$$ | 2-3 hours | Supertree Grove is free and magical at night during the light show. Cloud Forest and Flower Dome are paid but stunning. |
| Marina Bay Sands SkyPark | Views | Marina Bay | $$ | 1 hour | The iconic rooftop observation deck. The infinity pool is hotel guests only, but the SkyPark is open to all. |
| Merlion Park | Iconic | Marina Bay | Free | 30 min | The half-lion, half-fish statue spouting water. Quick photo stop with Marina Bay Sands in the background. |
| Sultan Mosque (Masjid Sultan) | Culture | Kampong Glam | Free | 30 min | Beautiful golden-domed mosque. The surrounding Haji Lane is Instagram gold with colorful murals. |
| Singapore Botanic Gardens | Nature | Tanglin | Free | 2-3 hours | UNESCO World Heritage Site. The National Orchid Garden (paid) has over 1,000 species. |
| Jewel Changi Airport | Architecture | Changi | Free | 1-2 hours | The world's best airport attraction. A 40-meter indoor waterfall surrounded by a forest. Worth a visit even without flying. |
| Sentosa Island | Leisure | Sentosa | $$-$$$ | Full day | Universal Studios, S.E.A. Aquarium, Siloso Beach. Family-friendly resort island connected by monorail. |
| Sri Mariamman Temple | Culture | Chinatown | Free | 30 min | Singapore's oldest Hindu temple with an ornate gopuram (entrance tower). In the heart of Chinatown. |
| National Gallery Singapore | Art | Civic District | $$ | 2-3 hours | Southeast Asian art collection in two beautifully restored colonial buildings. The rooftop bar has city views. |
| Night Safari | Nature | Mandai | $$ | 3 hours | The world's first nocturnal zoo. Tram rides through habitats, walking trails, creature encounters in the dark. |

## Nightlife

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Clarke Quay | Bars/clubs | Clarke Quay | $$-$$$ | Singapore's main nightlife strip along the river. Clubs, bars, and restaurants lit up with colorful lights. |
| Atlas | Gin bar | Bugis | $$$ | Soaring Art Deco lobby with the tallest gin tower in the world. A destination bar, not just a drink stop. |
| Lau Pa Sat Late Night Satay | Food/drinks | CBD | $ | After-hours satay stalls on Boon Tat Street. Cold Tiger beers and smoky skewers under the stars. |
| Zouk | Club | Clarke Quay | $$ | One of Asia's legendary nightclubs. Multiple rooms, top DJs, open until very late on weekends. |
| 28 HongKong Street | Speakeasy | Clarke Quay | $$-$$$ | No sign outside, world-ranked cocktail bar inside. Ask for a recommendation and trust the bartender. |

## Transit tips

- **MRT**: Clean, efficient, air-conditioned. Covers the entire island. Runs 5:30 AM to midnight.
- **EZ-Link card**: Rechargeable card for MRT and buses. Buy at any station or 7-Eleven.
- **Buses**: Excellent network. Google Maps works perfectly for bus routes in Singapore.
- **Grab**: The rideshare app for Southeast Asia. Affordable, reliable, air-conditioned.
- **Walking**: Very walkable but very hot. Use underground connectors between MRT stations and malls.
- **Pro tip**: Changi Airport is 20-30 minutes by MRT from the city. The airport itself has free tours, a butterfly garden, and a rooftop pool.
- **No gum**: Chewing gum is famously banned. Fines for littering are real. Singapore is strict but safe.

## Useful Singlish phrases

| English | Singlish | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| Can you do this? | Can or not? | Asking if something is possible |
| Yes, I can | Can lah | Affirmative, often enthusiastic |
| Let's eat | Makan | From Malay, means eat or food |
| Afraid of losing out | Kiasu | The national trait of not wanting to miss out |
| It's very good | Shiok! | Pleasure, satisfaction, deliciousness |
| What's this? | What is this one ah? | Curiosity with characteristic "ah" |
| No way | Cannot lah | Strong negative |
| Let's go | Jalan jalan | From Malay, means to stroll or go out |
